Deserved-Results Engine · 2022–2025

What drivers earned,
not what they got

Every Formula 1 result splits into the pace a driver showed, how they drove on the day, and the luck that moved them. Strip the luck out and you can rate drivers on what they actually earned.

A driver rating in the style of chess Elo, built only on luck-removed results. Each race, every driver is measured against the rest of the field: finish ahead of drivers you were expected to beat and you gain rating, finish behind them and you lose it, with bigger margins counting for more. Because it runs on finishing order with the luck stripped out, it rates drivers on what they earned, not what the results handed them. A crash a driver caused counts against them; a mechanical failure or getting taken out doesn't move the rating, since neither was their doing. Tap a driver to see their rating race by race, or hover any point on the chart for the race, result, and rating. Lines change color when a driver changes team.
Rating over 2022–2025
Solid line = higher-rated driver in each team, dotted = teammate. Hover a line to focus it.

On actual results
Events
The actual column is the real championship, sprint points included, and it matches the official result. Luck removed re-scores every race and sprint after taking luck out. The swing is points gained once luck is removed: positive means luck cost them, negative means luck helped. Tap a driver to see the race-by-race season behind their total. 2025 is the one to look at.
#
Driver
Actual
Luck removed
Move
Swing
Pick a race and see the whole field, sorted by how it should have gone with the luck removed. Each row shows where they started on the grid, where they actually finished, and their luck-removed position. The move column is how many places luck gave or took. Drivers who didn't finish are at the bottom with the reason. Sprints are included. Tap any driver for the full breakdown.

Luck rem.
Move
Expected is where their raw pace alone puts them. Race-day drive is how they actually drove against that. Put those together and take luck out and you get the earned result. Actual finish is that plus the luck. This ranks every drive by how much luck moved it.

Driver
Race
Expected → Finish
Luck swing
Net
Built on public data: FastF1 for lap timing, pit stops and stewards messages, F1DB for sprint results and points. Luck is measured in positions, relative to the field each race. The pace baseline is race pace versus teammate, not overall skill. Sprints count by their distance, about a third of a grand prix. 2022–2025.
